title: rubus fruticosus subsp. leucostachys
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. leucostachys, commonly known as wild raspberry or bramble, is a type of bramble plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, offering various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Rubus fruticosus subsp. leucostachys is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
